hook.text: An order references a customer by ID. To display the order, you need the customer's name. That's a second query.

buildup.text: The extended reference embeds frequently-read fields alongside the ID: { customer_id: 42, customer_name: 'Alice' }.

discovery.text: Now displaying the order requires one read. The customer name is right there in the order document.

twist.text: If Alice changes her name, you must update it in every order. Trade stale data risk for read performance.

climax.text: Only embed fields that rarely change: names, categories, labels. Not prices or stock counts.

punchline.text: Extended reference: copy stable fields to avoid extra lookups. Update on change.

quiz.question: What is the risk of the extended reference pattern?
options: Copied fields can become stale if the source data changes and the copies aren't updated
It uses twice as much disk space for every document
Extended references prevent all future schema changes
